title: festuca ovina f. frisia
intro: Festuca ovina f. frisia, also known as sheep's fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Festuca ovina f. frisia include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as having antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. In herbal preparations, Festuca ovina f. frisia is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
